response.step_by_step_log: 1. 00:00-01:13: Identified the song 'JAPANの美術史♪お・ぼ・え・ま・SHOW！' performed by 儒烏風亭らでん. 2. Searched for 'JAPANの美術史 儒烏風亭らでん 歌詞' and found the full lyrics. 3. 01:13 onwards: The rest of the video is an educational art history segment.
response.global_summary: A video featuring a musical performance of "JAPANの美術史♪お・ぼ・え・ま・SHOW！" by Hololive VTuber 儒烏風亭らでん (Juufuutei Raden), followed by an educational segment about Japanese art history.

scenes.description: A musical performance of the song 'JAPANの美術史♪お・ぼ・え・ま・SHOW！'. The performer, 儒烏風亭らでん, sings and dances while the lyrics appear on screen.
scenes.song_title: JAPANの美術史♪お・ぼ・え・ま・SHOW！
scenes.reference_lyrics_en:
JAPAN's Art History (Art! Art!) 
JAPAN's Art History (Paintings to 3D!) 
JAPAN's Art History (The test will be perfect too!) 
Let's learn! 
Hey everyone, hello! 
It's time to study art history! 
First, let's try starting from the Jomon, Yayoi, and Kofun periods 
Flame-style pottery, dogu with snow goggles, Jomon Venus! (Doki Doki) 
Dotaku, doka, dohoko, doken, Yayoi pottery (Doki Doki) 
Haniwa too, various ones, dancing people, keiko bujin, horse-shaped haniwa 
Iron swords, iron swords, shichishito, Haji pottery, Sue pottery 
OK OK OK OK feeling good! 
Next is the Asuka to Nara period! 
Three! Two! One! Go! 
Lots of Buddhist statues, Shaka Nyorai, together with Bodhisattvas, a triad 
Tamamushi-no-zushi, Tenjukoku Shucho, Kondo wall paintings, Kofun wall paintings 
In Todai-ji is the Great Buddha of Nara, in Kofuku-ji is the Ashura statue 
Let's play with sound! 
Raden Shitan no Gogen Biwa 
To reach the future you 
Let's connect this feeling 
As long as there is love, it will be alright 
Let's talk until the morning comes 
JAPAN's Art History (Art! Art!) 
JAPAN's Art History (Paintings to 3D!) 
JAPAN's Art History (The test will be perfect too!) 
Let's learn! 
There is still much more 
Please do your best to keep up 
Heian, Kamakura, Nanbokucho, here we come 
Mandala, ichiboku-zukuri, yosegi-zukuri 
Choju-jinbutsu-giga and The Tale of Genji 
Byodo-in Ho-o-do, Amida Nyorai zazou 
Patterns that are so cute, Katowaguruma raden tebako! 
Todai-ji's Nandaimon Kongo Rikishi statue 
Amida Niju-go Bosatsu Raigou-zu, Yamagoe Amida-zu 
Mo-ko Shu-rai E-kotoba, Kuya Shonin statue 
Ippen Shonin E-den, Gosannen Kassen E-maki 
Yo Yo! We've come about halfway, I guess! 
Let's keep going, as it is 
From Muromachi to Azuchi-Momoyama! 
Noh mask Okina and Karesansui 
Amanohashidate-zu, Ryodohin-zu 
Kano school folding screens! 
Yohen Tenmoku tea bowl 
Rakuchu Rakugai-zu folding screen 
Karajishi-zu folding screen, Matsutaka-zu 
Shokunin-zukushi-zu, Kaka Yuraku-zu 
Shorin-zu, Sansui-zu, Yatsuhashi Makie Raden Suzuribako 
To reach the future you 
Let's connect this feeling 
As long as there is love, it will be alright 
Let's talk until the morning comes 
Thank you for following this far! 
Just a little more, let's do our best until the end! 
From the Edo period to the Showa era! 
Here we go! Three, two, one, go! 
Funabashi Makie Suzuribako, Fujin Raijin-zu folding screen 
Kakitsubata-zu folding screen, Oiran (Mikaeri Bijin) 
Koshoku Ichidai Otoko, Oku no Hosomichi 
Fugaku Sanjurokkei, Tokaidochu Hizakurige 
Roen, Yuami, Kofu 
Bakamori, Gigeiten 
Ryuko-zu, Kuroki Neko 
Sake, Umi no Sachi 
Totou no Yugure 
Reiko Bishou, Shikinjo 
Han-neko, Izuura Churibito 
Taiyou no Tou 
Okamoto Taro's Circle (Yoshihara Jiro's Circle) 
Higashiyama Kaii's Road 
To reach the future you 
Let's connect this feeling 
As long as there is love, it will be alright 
Let's talk until the morning comes 
Until the future 
JAPAN's Art History (Art! Art!) 
JAPAN's Art History (Paintings to 3D!) 
JAPAN's Art History (The test will be perfect too!)

scenes.description: The performer explains art history in a Japanese-style room, focusing on a specific national treasure.
